game_name: Super Street Fighter II: The New Challengers

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character with unique moveset → engage opponent in best-of-three matches → execute special moves and combos → defeat increasingly difficult AI opponents or human players
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Match-up knowledge and execution pressure: player must learn character-specific framedata, spacing, and combo timings while adapting to opponent's fighting style in real-time
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a martial artist through practice and memorization; prove skill competitively against both AI and human opponents; discover character-specific playstyles and strategies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes mechanical depth over accessibility; introduces new scoring system rewarding combo execution and technical play (first attacks, reversals, recoveries); balances all 16 characters with intentional design choices (speed reductions from Hyper Fighting, special move refinements); positions fighting game as skill-based competitive medium rather than narrative-driven experience

difficulty.lives_system: Best-of-three rounds per match; matches continue until two rounds won
difficulty.checkpointing: None; single-elimination tournament structure with eight randomly-chosen opponents followed by four Grand Masters (Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison) in fixed order
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Genesis version offers selectable game speed levels; players can adjust training intensity through difficulty settings and opponent selection in various game modes

progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight new color schemes per character (original plus Champion Edition, Hyper Fighting, and five new schemes); four new playable characters (Cammy, T. Hawk, Dee Jay, Fei Long) available from start of game
replayability_hooks: 16 distinct character rosters with unique movesets and playstyles
Single-elimination tournament generates randomized opponent selection
Tournament Battle mode (Genesis) enabling eight-player networked play across four arcade cabinets
Multiple game modes (Arcade, Group Battle, Time Challenge, Tournament)

world_building_techniques: Character introductions through brief biographical descriptions (Cammy as special forces agent with M. Bison connection; T. Hawk as Native American warrior seeking ancestral land; Dee Jay as Jamaican kickboxing musician; Fei Long as Hong Kong movie star modeled after Bruce Lee)
Stage selection tied to character origins (implied through character nationality/profession)

boss_design.philosophy: Four Grand Masters (Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison) serve as final progression checkpoint after eight randomly-selected opponents; designed as skill-gate encounters testing mastery of character mechanics against fixed, predictable AI patterns
notable_bosses: M. Bison (final boss; connected to multiple character narratives including Cammy and T. Hawk's backstories)

faction_indicators: Shadaloo organization (M. Bison's entity; antagonistic force in T. Hawk and Cammy narratives)
International martial arts tournament competitors (unaligned individual fighters seeking competitive proof)
danger_signals: M. Bison's presence as recurring antagonist across multiple character stories
Cammy's mysterious past connection to Shadaloo
T. Hawk's dispossession and resistance narrative
environmental_storytelling: Character stage designs reflecting nationality/profession (implied but not detailed in source)
Character portraits and HUD graphics updated to reflect revised appearance across versions
